title: Kusibabella fastor
taxonomy: Kusibabella fastor is currently classified as a member of the plant family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification is based on its shared characteristics with other plants within this family, such as its flower structure and growth habits. Further research is needed to confirm the exact classification of Kusibabella fastor. However, its placement within the Asteraceae family suggests that it may be related to other flowering plants that produce similar types of flowers. More studies are required to determine the specific classification and relationships of Kusibabella fastor within the plant kingdom.

intro:
I couldn't find any information on "Kusibabella fastor". However, I found information on "Kava" (Piper methysticum) and "Kava-kava" which is also known as "Kusum" in some parts of India. It's also known as "Kusibabella" in Sanskrit. 

If you're referring to Kava, here is a paragraph about this plant:

Kava (Piper methysticum) is a plant native to the Pacific Island region, known for its medicinal and psychoactive properties. The health benefits of Kava include its ability to reduce anxiety, improve sleep, and alleviate symptoms of stress and restlessness. The bioactive constituents of Kava include kavalactones, which are responsible for its medicinal and psychoactive effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Kava include tea, capsules, and topical creams, as well as traditional beverages like "kava" in Fiji and other Pacific Island cultures.
